question: How does StreamDock VSD Craft software on Windows launch plugin entry points? Specifically: what does CodePathWin in manifest.json typically point to (a .exe directly, a .bat/.cmd wrapper that invokes node.exe, a .ps1, something else)? Can we keep our existing Node.js main.js and just change the entry to a .cmd wrapper that invokes node.exe? Does the host bundle a Node runtime, or must the user have Node installed system-wide?
constraints: Preserve the existing main.js + sdk.js + vendored ws (don't rewrite the plugin process in C#/Rust unless absolutely necessary).
User has VSD Craft installed at C:\Program Files (x86)\VSD Craft\; plugin folder is C:\Program Files (x86)\VSD Craft\plugins.
We don't know whether the user has Node.js installed system-wide. If they don't, we may need to bundle a portable Node or use a different runtime.
